reviews.review_description: Awesomeness
If you loved firefox vr, be sure to checkout wolvic, it’s the official continuation of this project and it’s still getting updated. I like it, way better than the default browser
Discontinued App.
Tried different vr modes not show movie or clip just sound...help

I don't understand,  why they can just delete my saved files without my permission.  Totally 15G, I bought videos, now it's gone. You told me earlier,  I would have saved them in other place. I am pissed off.

reviews.review_title: Only way to view Matterport on Quest atm
reviews.review_description: Oculus' browser wasn't recognized by Matterport when I tried view a virtual real estate tour stored there. Firefox Reality lets you do the tour, opening full VR mode, letting you even use controllers to navigate.
Every time I open it the browser opens up behind me and not in front of me. And I cant move it to where I need it to
Good browser, but not 8k video 360
It's very nice, however I have two issues. I would like to see multi handed typing added to make typing easier. Also, VR video seems to lag when switching resolutions.
Netflix doesn't work.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback! Unfortunately, Netflix blocks access to their service from web browsers on mobile platforms, including the Oculus Quest.

reviews.review_description:
Please let us open at least 5 to 7 tabs.
Thank you.

Doesn't work at all. You'll get a full black screen, or just a spinning icon. Tried for a couple of hours to troubleshoot, but gave up. Hard to believe that after this much time, it's still worthless. Makes me want to think of changing my default browser now.

Firefox is specialized in keeping you safe while browsing. It does its best to give you a optimal browsing experience while also keeping you safe from unwanted websites using cookies it even has add-ons that can improve safety

Browser itself seems to work fine. Video playback is completely broken. Trying to play a video in VR mode results in it being rendered as a tiny disfigured video somewhere high above you. It's downloads are somewhere under the Android system directory as opposed to the actual Downloads folder. Worst part, I lost around 30 GB of downloads, which magically disappeared a day later. Extremely frustrating. Never using this again

Every VR video I watch on this freezes or plays very choppy.  The native browser on the oculus is much better at playing videos but it has other issues.  If you can get the video playback issues fixed I’d be willing to pay for this but until then it’s not worth using even for free.

developer_response.body: I'm sorry to hear that! Please try the latest version, which has several updates and fixes to common video playback issues.

reviews.review_description: It has a strange zoom and feels slow. Standard browser is good enough.

reviews.review_title: Beats Oculus Browser in one way
reviews.review_description: I'm able to run a webpage-based Webex Meeting through this browser and not the default Oculus Browser. Oculus Browser only let me run the page as a VR or mobile client, not as a desktop client. Firefox did let me, and that was how I was able to join a Webex meeting on my Oculus. So, this browser is worth having around in case you visit some complex web pages that require a bit more of a desktop-like set of technologies or experience.
If devs won’t keep a web browser up to date they should remove it from the store.

All the modern functionality is great, plus the right click open in new tab/window by long pressing works great. BUT THE VR VIDEOS WON’T PLAY MOST OF THE TIME!!!! I got it to work occasionally but it doesn’t work most of time!! Please fix it. Please.
I use Firefox on everything but Mac, so it's great I can transfer all of my data to my Quest. I personally prefer this to the native browser, and I recommend you to try it as well.
